title: Nephrology referral documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports careful medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, ACR, electrolyte, and imaging context in clinician-led CKD evaluation.
examination.rationale: Supports evaluation of cause and kidney-function context for nephrology referral documentation; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports careful medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, ACR, electrolyte, and imaging context in clinician-led CKD evaluation.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Evaluation of cause and kidney-function context
exact_section.locator: Practice Point 1.1.4.1, Figure 8, Table 6, and Practice Point 1.2.1.1; summary PDF pages 34–35 and 61

referral.text: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.
referral.rationale: Supports qualified referral to specialist kidney-care services for nephrology referral documentation; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Referral to specialist kidney care services
exact_section.locator: Practice Point 5.1.1 and Figure 48; summary PDF pages 51 and 140–141

referral.text: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.
referral.rationale: Supports GFR and albuminuria monitoring context for nephrology referral documentation; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Monitoring GFR and albuminuria in CKD
exact_section.locator: Practice Points 2.1.1–2.1.5; summary PDF pages 40 and 81–82

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Reason, urgency, cause, GFR and ACR trends, urine findings, complications, medicines, investigations, consent, destination and safety plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for nephrology referral documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
